name: wp17-csv-typed-loader-slice
goal: Use a real product slice to calibrate practical runPolicy guardrails: start CSV support as a bounded typed adapter without broadening the engine story.

sharedContext.summary: Start Phase 1 of the CSV spike with the smallest valuable adapter slice: load UTF-8 CSV into typed rows, keep CSV at the file boundary, and reuse the existing in-memory engine after load.
constraints: Keep CSV support adapter-only and typed-first; do not turn the repo into a dataframe, ETL, or generic tabular platform.
Prefer the smallest actor set that still lowers risk: one implementer task plus one reviewer task.
Do not edit TODO.md, ai/state files, ai/log files, or ai/indexes.

tasks.title: Implement the first typed CSV adapter slice
tasks.prompt: Implement the smallest valuable CSV starter slice described in SPIKE-CSV.md. Add a bounded public PojoLensCsv entry point, narrow CsvOptions, strict typed header-based loading with row/column-aware errors, focused tests, and only the documentation needed to frame this as a boundary adapter into the existing engine.

tasks.title: Review the CSV boundary slice
tasks.prompt: Review the dependency output and inspect the changed CSV files. Focus on scope creep, missing validation, public-surface risks, and any place where the adapter starts looking like a generic table platform instead of a bounded CSV-to-engine bridge.
